title: ranunculus acris var. japonicus
intro: Ranunculus acris var. japonicus, also known as Japanese buttercup, is a variety of buttercup plant native to Japan, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Japanese buttercup include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as eczema and acne. Japanese buttercup is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
